convention: anthropics/skills/skill-creator evals.json — trigger-only fixture per references/schemas.md. Each entry carries `should_trigger` so skill-creator's description-optimisation loop can score train/held-out trigger accuracy.
notes: Positives target v1->v2 migration prompts: explicit phrasing OR any v1 surface mention (re-frame.db, dispatch-with, reg-global-interceptor, reg-sub-raw, ^:flush-dom, re-frame.alpha, re-frame-test, old top-level :dispatch / :dispatch-n effect-map keys). Negatives target greenfield, authoring on already-v2, live-runtime, and pure spec / EP questions.

evals.name: explicit-migrate
evals.prompt: Migrate my re-frame v1 app to re-frame2.

evals.name: explicit-upgrade
evals.prompt: Upgrade my codebase from re-frame to re-frame2.

evals.name: v1-to-v2
evals.prompt: What changes from v1 to v2? Walk me through what breaks.

evals.name: v1-surface-db
evals.prompt: I have a lot of code that imports re-frame.db directly. How does that work under re-frame2?

evals.name: v1-surface-dispatch
evals.prompt: Our codebase uses dispatch-with and reg-global-interceptor heavily — what's the v2 equivalent?

evals.name: v1-surface-flush-dom
evals.prompt: We rely on ^:flush-dom in a bunch of places. Is that still a thing in re-frame2?

evals.name: v1-surface-test
evals.prompt: Our tests use re-frame-test extensively. What replaces it in v2?

evals.name: v1-effect-map-keys
evals.prompt: Our handlers return {:db ... :dispatch [...] :dispatch-n [[...]]}. Does v2 still accept those top-level keys?

evals.name: neg-greenfield
evals.prompt: Start a new re-frame2 project from scratch.

evals.name: neg-author-v2
evals.prompt: Write a re-frame2 reg-event-fx that posts a login form to /api/login.
evals.rationale: Authoring on an already-v2 project is re-frame2.

evals.name: neg-live-pair
evals.prompt: Inspect app-db right now on my running shadow-cljs build.

evals.name: neg-port-language
evals.prompt: I want to port re-frame2 to F#.

evals.name: neg-spec-question
evals.prompt: What's the EP rationale for treating effects as data in v2?

evals.name: neg-causa-tour
evals.prompt: Which Causa panel shows my schema violations?
evals.rationale: Causa tour is its own skill.

evals.name: neg-improver
evals.prompt: Review events.cljs in my v2 project for anti-patterns.

evals.name: neg-vocab-migrate-db
evals.prompt: Migrate my Postgres database from 12 to 14.
evals.rationale: Vocabulary hit ('migrate') on an unrelated surface.
